title: Fermented Quick Brazilian Salsa Agemono
description: A fast and flavorful Brazilian dish featuring a fermented salsa and crispy agemono-deep-fried goodness, suitable for a quick fix and fodmap-friendly.
instructions: In a blender or food processor, blend the crushed tomatoes, red onion, cilantro, parsley, garlic, kosher salt, and black pepper until smooth.
In a small bowl, mix the white vinegar and rice vinegar, and stir until dissolved.
Pour the vinegar mixture into the blender or food processor and blend until combined.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and let it ferment at room temperature (around 75°F/24°C) for 2-3 days. Shake the jar daily to help the fermentation process.
Heat the extra virgin olive oil in a deep frying pan to 350°F (180°C) for the agemono-deep-frying.
Using a slotted spoon, carefully place the fermented salsa mixture into the hot oil and fry until golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
Using a paper towel-lined plate, drain the fried salsa mixture and let it cool slightly before serving.
